# ADR-260012: Approve Google Analytics (GA4) integration for the public site

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-06-16
Owners: Repository owner

## Context

ADR-260004 set the initial launch boundaries and deliberately excluded analytics
from the first public release — *"The initial launch will focus on core content
and functionality, with external integrations like analytics planned for a later
phase"* — to keep privacy/compliance and delivery scope simple during launch.
That ADR's own Rollback clause anticipated this follow-up:

> If these boundaries prove too strict, adjust with a follow-up ADR and add a
> scoped backlog item for the exception (e.g., approved analytics integration).

The public site has shipped. We now want visibility into traffic and page
engagement, so the deferred "later phase" for analytics has arrived. This ADR is
that approval. It supersedes **only** the analytics-launch clause of ADR-260004;
all other boundaries in ADR-260004 (copy policy, form-backend exclusion, contact
`mailto:` fallback, redirect policy, visual reuse) remain in force.

## Decision

- **Approve Google Analytics 4** (gtag.js) on the public site, measurement ID
  `G-CS37QHFW49`.
- The official gtag.js snippet is rendered from a single shared component,
  `src/components/Analytics.astro`, and included in the `<head>` of both
  `src/layouts/MainLayout.astro` and `src/layouts/AddonLayout.astro` so every
  content page is covered. Redirect-only stubs (`/landing`, `/gaddon`) are not
  required to carry it.
- **The official snippet is used verbatim and must not be refactored** (e.g. into
  rest-params), per LRN-002 — a rest-params rewrite silently sends zero data.
- The form-backend prohibition and all other ADR-260004 boundaries are unchanged.
- The boundary tests (`e2e/boundaries.spec.ts`, `src/utils/boundaries.test.ts`)
  are updated to **require** the GA tag on content pages instead of forbidding it,
  while continuing to block external form-backend patterns.

## Alternatives Considered

- **Amend ADR-260004 in place / reopen BI-260004**: Rejected. ADR-260004 was
  correct for the launch phase and BI-260004 shipped as specified; mutating an
  Accepted record or reopening a Done item would rewrite settled history and
  prescribes its own rollback as a *follow-up* ADR. A scoped superseding ADR is
  the governance-consistent path.
- **Self-hosted / privacy-first analytics (Plausible, Umami)**: Reasonable on
  privacy grounds but out of scope for this decision; GA4 is the immediately
  available tool. A future ADR may revisit the analytics vendor.
- **Keep analytics off**: Rejected; the launch-phase reason for deferral no
  longer applies now that the site is live and traffic insight is wanted.

## Consequences

- Positive: Traffic and engagement data become available via GA4 Realtime/Reports.
- Positive: Centralizing the snippet in one component keeps both layouts in sync
  and gives a single place to manage the measurement ID.
- Negative: Adds a third-party script (`googletagmanager.com`) to content pages;
  a future CSP configuration must allow it (compounds the GSI note in ADR-260011).
- Negative: Privacy/compliance surface grows (cookie/consent considerations may
  need a follow-up if regulatory scope requires it).

## Verification

- Content pages emit the gtag snippet; the boundary tests assert its presence.
- The official snippet is unmodified (`function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);}`).
- The `collect` request returns `204` from a clean (unblocked) browser, confirmed
  in GA Realtime — see LRN-002 for the verification procedure.

## Rollback

Remove `src/components/Analytics.astro` and its inclusion from both layouts, and
revert the boundary tests to forbid analytics. ADR-260004's analytics clause then
returns to effect.

- Related backlog item(s): BI-260012.md
- Supersedes (analytics-launch clause only): ADR-260004.md §Decision "Launch integration policy"
- Governed by: ADR-260004.md (remaining boundary policy)
- Related learning: ../learnings/LRN-002-google-analytics-ga4-setup.md
